title: Branchlessly restore the free memory pointer using computed mstore destination
description: To conditionally restore the free memory pointer without a branch: compute the mstore destination as either 0x40 (the free pointer location) or 0x00 (scratch space, a no-op write). shl(6, condition) produces 64 (0x40) when condition=1, and 0 when condition=0. The condition checks whether the buffer is still at the top of allocated memory (safe to reclaim). This avoids a JUMPI opcode.
when_to_apply: Temporary buffer deallocation in assembly where memory may or may not have been allocated after the buffer
when_not_to_apply: When the buffer is always the last allocation (unconditional restore is simpler)
